req.title: Submit timesheet — variant 2
req.statement: An employee shall submit a weekly timesheet where each day has hours between 0 and 24 and total week hours are between 0 and 80. Additionally, the action must be idempotent under retry.

doc.reasoning: The requirement defines three orthogonal validation zones: (1) valid per-day hours [0,24] and valid weekly total [0,80] — covered by EP positive and BVA boundary tests; (2) per-day hours outside [0,24] and weekly total outside [0,80] — covered by NEG/DT negative tests; (3) idempotency under retry — covered by STC test modelling the state transition draft→submitted→submitted(retry). BVA targets the exact boundary values 0, 24, 80 because off-by-one errors are the primary defect risk. DT covers the compound invalid condition (both rules violated simultaneously). STC verifies that a second identical POST does not change state or produce an error.

tests.name: test_submit_valid_timesheet_accepted
preconditions: Employee is authenticated (valid session token available via <auth_fixture>)
No previously submitted timesheet exists for the target week
actions: Build a payload where each of the 7 days has hours within (0, 24) and the weekly total is within (0, 80)
POST payload to /timesheets/submit with auth header
Capture HTTP response
expected: Response body contains timesheet state equal to 'submitted'

tests.name: test_submit_timesheet_per_day_hours_exceeds_24_returns_422
preconditions: Employee is authenticated (valid session token available via <auth_fixture>)
Target week has no previously submitted timesheet
actions: Build a payload where Monday has 25 hours (1 above the per-day maximum)
Ensure weekly total would also exceed 80 to combine both violations for DT coverage
POST payload to /timesheets/submit with auth header
Capture HTTP response
expected: Response body contains a validation error referencing per-day hours constraint
trace: R-WEB-068: error pathway — 422 when per-day hours outside 0-24
R-WEB-068: DT — both per-day and weekly constraints violated simultaneously

tests.name: test_submit_timesheet_weekly_total_exceeds_80_returns_422
preconditions: Employee is authenticated (valid session token available via <auth_fixture>)
Target week has no previously submitted timesheet
Each individual day value is valid (<= 24)
actions: Build a payload where each day has exactly 12 hours (7 * 12 = 84 > 80) but per-day value is within [0,24]
POST payload to /timesheets/submit with auth header
Capture HTTP response
expected: Response body contains a validation error referencing weekly total constraint
trace: R-WEB-068: error pathway — 422 when weekly total outside 0-80
R-WEB-068: DT — per-day valid but weekly total invalid (independent rule)

tests.name: test_submit_timesheet_boundary_max_valid_hours
preconditions: Employee is authenticated (valid session token available via <auth_fixture>)
Target week has no previously submitted timesheet
actions: Build a payload: Mon-Fri each exactly 16 hours (total = 80), Sat=Sun=0 — hits both per-day upper safe value and weekly maximum exactly
POST payload to /timesheets/submit with auth header
Capture HTTP response
expected: Response body contains timesheet state equal to 'submitted'
trace: R-WEB-068: BVA — weekly total exactly at upper boundary (80)
R-WEB-068: BVA — per-day hours well within upper boundary (16 < 24)

tests.name: test_submit_timesheet_boundary_zero_hours_accepted
preconditions: Employee is authenticated (valid session token available via <auth_fixture>)
Target week has no previously submitted timesheet
actions: Build a payload where all 7 days have exactly 0 hours (lower boundary for both constraints)
POST payload to /timesheets/submit with auth header
Capture HTTP response
expected: Response body contains timesheet state equal to 'submitted'
trace: R-WEB-068: BVA — per-day hours exactly at lower boundary (0)
R-WEB-068: BVA — weekly total exactly at lower boundary (0)

tests.name: test_submit_timesheet_idempotent_on_retry
preconditions: Employee is authenticated (valid session token available via <auth_fixture>)
Target week has no previously submitted timesheet (state=draft)
actions: POST a valid timesheet payload to /timesheets/submit — first submission (state: draft -> submitted)
Assert first response indicates state=submitted
POST the identical payload again — retry (state: submitted -> submitted, idempotent)
Assert second response also indicates state=submitted without error
expected: First POST returns 200 or 201 with state=submitted
Second POST returns 200 or 201 with state=submitted (not 409 or 422)
State does not regress or duplicate
trace: R-WEB-068: additional variant semantics — action must be idempotent under retry
